new_classes.definition_en: A spirit manufactured from sugar-cane molasses or from molasses of the sweet juices of plants and of rice, using a special yeast. Arrack is distinct from raki, which is obtained by redistilling raisin or dry fig spirits with aniseed.
A distillation process in which a special yeast is used to ferment sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant juice molasses together with rice, yielding arrack.
Molasses derived from sugar cane or from sweet juices of plants, used as a fermentation feedstock in the production of arrack.
Rice used as a fermentation feedstock alongside sugar-cane or plant molasses in the production of arrack.
A specific yeast strain used in the fermentation step of arrack production, distinguishing arrack from other sugar-cane or molasses-based spirits.

new_classes.label_de: Arrak in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von höchstens 2 Litern (KN 2208 90 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Arrack presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2208 90 11.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria for arrack from the legal text: (1) production from sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant juice molasses and rice, (2) use of a special yeast, (3) classification as a spiritous beverage of heading 2208, and (4) container volume ≤ 2 L for subheading 22089011. A disjointness complement restriction is included to distinguish arrack from plain fermented sugar-cane spirits (rum). The distinction from raki (subheading 22089056/22089099) is noted in the legal text but cannot be fully captured by the current TBox as no Raki class exists yet; this gap reduces coverage slightly. The complement restriction on hasDistillationFeedstock is approximate since raki's aniseed/raisin/fig feedstock cannot be expressed negatively without a dedicated class. Overall coverage is approximately 0.85.
